channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: TDA-RC: Task-Driven Alignment for Knowledge-Based Reasoning Chains in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.04942v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enhancing the reasoning capability of large language models (LLMs) remains a core challenge in natural language processing. The Chain-of-Thought (CoT) paradigm dominates practical applications for its single-round efficiency, yet its reasoning chains ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, LLM, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Inclusion-of-Thoughts: Mitigating Preference Instability via Purifying the Decision Space
item.description: arXiv:2604.04944v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multiple-choice questions (MCQs) are widely used to evaluate large language models (LLMs). However, LLMs remain vulnerable to the presence of plausible distractors. This often diverts attention toward irrelevant choices, resulting in unstable oscillat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, chain-of-thought, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Phase-Associative Memory: Sequence Modeling in Complex Hilbert Space
item.description: arXiv:2604.05030v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Phase-Associative Memory (PAM), a recurrent sequence model in which all representations are complex-valued, associations accumulate in a matrix state $S_{t}$ $\in$ $\mathbb{C}^{d \times d}$ via outer products, and retrieval operates through...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vector, memory, arxiv, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: This Treatment Works, Right? Evaluating LLM Sensitivity to Patient Question Framing in Medical QA
item.description: arXiv:2604.05051v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Patients are increasingly turning to large language models (LLMs) with medical questions that are complex and difficult to articulate clearly. However, LLMs are sensitive to prompt phrasings and can be influenced by the way questions are worded. Ideal...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, retrieval, LLM, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond LLM-as-a-Judge: Deterministic Metrics for Multilingual Generative Text Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2604.05083v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly adopted as automated judges for evaluating generated text, their outputs are often costly, and highly sensitive to prompt design, language, and aggregation strategies, severely, which limits reproduc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vision, arxiv, LLM, summarization | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Document Optimization for Black-Box Retrieval via Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.05087v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Document expansion is a classical technique for improving retrieval quality, and is attractive since it shifts computation offline, avoiding additional query-time processing. However, when applied to modern retrievers, it has been shown to degrade per...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vector, vision, example, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MegaTrain: Full Precision Training of 100B+ Parameter Large Language Models on a Single GPU
item.description: arXiv:2604.05091v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present MegaTrain, a memory-centric system that efficiently trains 100B+ parameter large language models at full precision on a single GPU. Unlike traditional GPU-centric systems, MegaTrain stores parameters and optimizer states in host memory (CPU...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, memory, arxiv, template, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Pramana: Fine-Tuning Large Language Models for Epistemic Reasoning through Navya-Nyaya
item.description: arXiv:2604.04937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models produce fluent text but struggle with systematic reasoning, often hallucinating confident but unfounded claims. When Apple researchers added irrelevant context to mathematical problems, LLM performance degraded by 65% Apple Machi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, LLM, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ReVEL: Multi-Turn Reflective LLM-Guided Heuristic Evolution via Structured Performance Feedback
item.description: arXiv:2604.04940v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Designing effective heuristics for NP-hard combinatorial optimization problems remains a challenging and expertise-intensive task. Existing applications of large language models (LLMs) primarily rely on one-shot code synthesis, yielding brittle heuris...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, LLM, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MedGemma 1.5 Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2604.05081v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce MedGemma 1.5 4B, the latest model in the MedGemma collection. MedGemma 1.5 expands on MedGemma 1 by integrating additional capabilities: high-dimensional medical imaging (CT/MRI volumes and histopathology whole slide images), anatomical l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, image, analysis, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Uncertainty-Guided Latent Diagnostic Trajectory Learning for Sequential Clinical Diagnosis
item.description: arXiv:2604.05116v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clinical diagnosis requires sequential evidence acquisition under uncertainty. However, most Large Language Model (LLM) based diagnostic systems assume fully observed patient information and therefore do not explicitly model how clinical evidence shou...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vision, framework, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Scaling DPPs for RAG: Density Meets Diversity
item.description: arXiv:2604.03240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances Large Language Models (LLMs) by grounding generation in external knowledge, yielding relevance responses that are aligned with factual evidence and evolving corpora. Standard RAG pipelines construct contex...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, augmented, study, arxiv, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DRAFT: Task Decoupled Latent Reasoning for Agent Safety
item.description: arXiv:2604.03242v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The advent of tool-using LLM agents shifts safety monitoring from output moderation to auditing long, noisy interaction trajectories, where risk-critical evidence is sparse-making standard binary supervision poorly suited for credit assignment. To add...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, framework, arxiv, tool, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NativeTernary: A Self-Delimiting Binary Encoding with Unary Run-Length Hierarchy Markers for Ternary Neural Network Weights, Structured Data, and General Computing Infrastructure
item.description: arXiv:2604.03336v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: BitNet b1.58 (Ma et al., 2024) demonstrates that large language models can operate entirely on ternary weights {-1, 0, +1}, yet no native binary wire format exists for such models. NativeTernary closes this gap. We present NativeTernary, a binary enco...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, RAG, large language model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The limits of bio-molecular modeling with large language models : a cross-scale evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2604.03361v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The modeling of bio-molecular system across molecular scales remains a central challenge in scientific research. Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to bio-molecular discovery, yet systematic evaluation across multi-scale biological ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, tool, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, prompt engineering, context, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, audio, chain-of-thought, reasoning, CoT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, RAG, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vector, framework, retrieval, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, framework, LLM, prompt, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: The Illusion of Latent Generalization: Bi-directionality and the Reversal Curse
item.description: arXiv:2604.04943v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The reversal curse describes a failure of autoregressive language models to retrieve a fact in reverse order (e.g., training on ``$A > B$'' but failing on ``$B < A$''). Recent work shows that objectives with bidirectional supervision (e.g., bidirectio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vision, study, arxiv, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PaperOrchestra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated AI Research Paper Writing
item.description: arXiv:2604.05018v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Synthesizing unstructured research materials into manuscripts is an essential yet under-explored challenge in AI-driven scientific discovery. Existing autonomous writers are rigidly coupled to specific experimental pipelines, and produce superficial l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, research, paper, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Part-Level 3D Gaussian Vehicle Generation with Joint and Hinge Axis Estimation
item.description: arXiv:2604.05070v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Simulation is essential for autonomous driving, yet current frameworks often model vehicles as rigid assets and fail to capture part-level articulation. With perception algorithms increasingly leveraging dynamics such as wheel steering or door opening...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, image, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Integrating Artificial Intelligence, Physics, and Internet of Things: A Framework for Cultural Heritage Conservation
item.description: arXiv:2604.03233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The conservation of cultural heritage increasingly relies on integrating technological innovation with domain expertise to ensure effective monitoring and predictive maintenance. This paper presents a novel framework to support the preservation of cul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, tool, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: General Explicit Network (GEN): A novel deep learning architecture for solving partial differential equations
item.description: arXiv:2604.03321v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning, especially physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) and their neural network variants, has been widely used to solve problems involving partial differential equations (PDEs). The successful deployment of such methods beyond academic ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, arxiv, research, paper, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Intelligent Energy Security: A Unified Spatio-Temporal and Graph Learning Framework for Scalable Electricity Theft Detection in Smart Grids
item.description: arXiv:2604.03344v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electricity theft and non-technical losses (NTLs) remain critical challenges in modern smart grids, causing significant economic losses and compromising grid reliability. This study introduces the SmartGuard Energy Intelligence System (SGEIS), an inte...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, memory, framework, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Model-Based Screening to Data-Driven Surrogates: A Multi-Stage Workflow for Exploring Stochastic Agent-Based Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.03350v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Systematic exploration of Agent-Based Models (ABMs) is challenged by the curse of dimensionality and their inherent stochasticity. We present a multi-stage pipeline integrating the systematic design of experiments with machine learning surrogates. Usi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, study, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, chain-of-thought, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: Memory Dial: A Training Framework for Controllable Memorization in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.05074v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Memorization in language models is widely studied but remains difficult to isolate and control. Understanding when and what models memorize is essential for explaining their predictions, yet existing approaches are post-hoc: they can detect memorizati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, memory, framework, study, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multilingual Language Models Encode Script Over Linguistic Structure
item.description: arXiv:2604.05090v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multilingual language models (LMs) organize representations for typologically and orthographically diverse languages into a shared parameter space, yet the nature of this internal organization remains elusive. In this work, we investigate which lingui...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Algebraic Structure Discovery for Real World Combinatorial Optimisation Problems: A General Framework from Abstract Algebra to Quotient Space Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.04941v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many combinatorial optimisation problems hide algebraic structures that, once exposed, shrink the search space and improve the chance of finding the global optimal solution. We present a general framework that (i) identifies algebraic structure, (ii) ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MMORF: A Multi-agent Framework for Designing Multi-objective Retrosynthesis Planning Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.05075v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-objective retrosynthesis planning is a critical chemistry task requiring dynamic balancing of quality, safety, and cost objectives. Language model-based multi-agent systems (MAS) offer a promising approach for this task: leveraging interactions ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Apparent Age Estimation: Challenges and Outcomes
item.description: arXiv:2604.03335v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Apparent age estimation is a valuable tool for business personalization, yet current models frequently exhibit demographic biases. We review prior works on the DEX method by applying distribution learning techniques such as Mean-Variance Loss (MVL) an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, embedding, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, tool, prompt engineering, prompt, step-by-step | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: Hardware-Oriented Inference Complexity of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
